meta.title: Nikolas Filippatos - Portfolio
meta.description: Cybersecurity Research Associate, focused on offensive security, training, and CTF tooling.

intro.description: I'm a Cybersecurity Research Associate at the University of Patras, working on research and tooling around network security, honeypot development, and hands-on offensive/defensive experimentation. My work spans containerized testbeds (Docker/Kubernetes), Linux and network security, and building automation in Python. I’m also active in the CTF community-participating, organizing events, and creating challenges, with a focus on miscellaneous and forensics. If you’re interested in collaborating on research, security engineering, or educational content, feel free to reach out.
more: Cybersecurity Research Associate at University of Patras, focused on 5G security, training, and CTF tooling.
Experience with containerized testbeds (Docker/Kubernetes), Linux/network security, and Python automation.
Active in the CTF community: organizing, participating, and creating challenges (misc/forensics).
Open to research, security engineering, or educational collaborations.

items.title: Cybersecurity Research Associate
description: Research and engineering for security experimentation and training environments
Contribute to technical documentation, demos, and research outputs
Build automation and tooling in Python; containerize deployments with Docker/Kubernetes
description_more: My work focuses on turning research output into repeatable lab environments and practical demonstrations that other researchers or students can actually use.

items.title: Lab Assistant - Database Management
description: Supervised weekly labs for 40 students, assisting with SQL and database concepts
Provided on-the-spot debugging, problem-solving and guidance

items.title: Lab Assistant - Website Development
description: Led 2-hour weekly labs for 40+ students covering Node.js, Handlebars, CSS and JavaScript
Answered technical questions and supported project development

items.title: A large scale analysis of code security in public repositories
items.description: This paper explores the code analysis features of the proposed platform were used in a large scale analysis on 10,855 public repositories.

items.title: A Practical Assessment of IDS and IPS Capabilities in a 5G Network Testbed
items.description: This paper presents a comprehensive assessment of Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) and Intrusion Prevention Systems (IPS) within a 5G network testbed. We evaluate the performance, accuracy, and effectiveness of various IDS/IPS solutions in detecting and mitigating cyber threats specific to 5G environments.

items.title: CTFSolver
items.description: CTFSolver is a tool designed to assist in solving CTF challenges. It provides utilities as a cli tool and a class for automating the solutions. It is currently being developed but can be installed via pypi ( ctfsolver )
description_more: The idea is to package repetitive solving workflows into reusable tooling, so challenge work can focus more on analysis and less on boilerplate setup.

items.title: SBOMPY
items.description: SBOMPY is a Python-based, FastAPI service that can be triggered via HTTP to generate SBOMs for the Docker workloads currently running on a host. It is designed for platform-style deployments where verticals are deployed as containers and must be scanned and recorded as part of the operational flow

items.title: Suricata Docker SAND5G
items.description: A Dockerized Suricata setup for IDS/IPS experimentation in the SAND5G context, intended for container-based network security testing and monitoring workflows.

items.title: FileAgent SAND5G
items.description: A Python and FastAPI-based file agent for managing custom Snort rules in a containerized environment, including file uploads, rule translation, backups, and duplicate prevention.

items.title: Snort Docker SAND5G
items.description: A Docker image and compose-based deployment for Snort 3, built for containerized IDS/IPS usage with custom rules, alert output, and automated rule reload workflows.

items.title: IOT Project
items.description: An infrastructure managing IOT tracker and buttons, connected to Fiware. It consists of 2 servers used for IOT communication, an express website server, a temporary server for the database and an MQTT publisher for virtual sensors.

items.title: Simulating Attack on a Computer System
items.description: A vulnerability assessment of a virtual machine. To gain access to the machine, four steps were followed: enumeration of the ports, identifying vulnerabilities, exploiting the vulnerabilities and elevating privileges for root user access.

items.title: Web Server Hardening
items.description: Setting up and securing a Linux web server. Using a virtual machine, set up a Linux web server using a headless Debian distro, configured the firewall and a fail2ban system to prevent brute force attacks, learned basic concepts of private-public key cryptography for more secure communication, set up a cloud instance, configured a custom DNS server and attempted a DNS caching attack from an external attacker's point of view.

items.title: Fullstack website Development: Bilbo
items.description: Bilbo is a website managing libraries. Developed using Node.js, Handlebars and SQLite as the database model.

items.title: Advanced Programming Techniques Project
items.description: A three part project used to demonstrate code testing, version control with git and GitHub, and dockerization using an MQTT server.

items.title: Database Project
items.description: A command line application managing charging stations for electrical cars. The database model is written in SQLite; the application and database management are in Python.

items.title: CTF Challenges
items.description: Created Capture The Flag challenges in the categories Miscellaneous and Forensics to teach practical cybersecurity skills.

achievements.title: Highlights
description: Executive Chair - IEEE Student Branch University of Patras (team of 25+ volunteers)
Cybersecurity Study Group Coordinator - IEEE SB UPatras (hands-on training & mentoring)
CTF Challenge Author & Organizer - UniHack 2025 CTF (University of Patras)
Selected competition results:
GRCSC CTF Competition 2025 - 2nd
ReplyCode Challenge 2025 - 249th
HTB Cyber Apocalypse CTF Competition 2025 - 434th
NTUA Hack3.0 CTF Competition - 2nd
University CTF 2024: Binary Badlands - 125th
NTUA Hack2.0 CTF Competition - 2nd

items.title: Hack The Box Meetup Host, Patra
description: Organizing meetups in Patra for the local Cybersecurity community. Coordinating hands-on workshops, live demonstrations and CTF-Style sessions focused on practical ethical hacking skills
https://www.meetup.com/hack-the-box-meetup-patras-gr

items.title: Event Organizer and author
description: Organized the Unihack 2025 CTF event, managing logistics and participant coordination in the University of Patras
Authored 6 CTF challenges in forensics and miscellaneous topics for the event
https://huh.ctflib.eu/

items.title: Executive Chair
items.company: IEEE Student Branch University of Patras
description: Managed 25 volunteers within a 100+ member branch
Organized events, external collaborations and community initiatives

As the Chair of the student branch, I am in charge of organizing and overseeing the executive committee(5 positions) , representing the branch in public events ,managing external affairs and keeping track of the overall progress of the organization .
description_more: The role also involved coordinating with different student groups, maintaining continuity across events, and supporting community growth over a longer period.

items.title: Cybersecurity Study Group Coordinator
items.company: IEEE Student Branch, University of Patras
description: Led sessions for 15 students on vulnerability discovery and exploitation
Prepared hands-on labs and guided practical cybersecurity training

In the scientific group we are learning about cybersecurity,identifying vulnerabilities and exploiting them, learning tools and techniques through cybersecurity challenges, such as ctf, tryhackme, hackthebox .

As a coordinator I am in charge of preparing for the meeting, finding useful material and challenges , presenting them , helping the group progress and assign homework to further the group's progress.

items.title: Competitive Programming Coordinator
items.company: IEEE Student Branch, University of Patras
description: Coordinated weekly algorithmic problem-solving sessions for 20 students
Prepared participants for national and international programming contests
Coordinated meetings to learn about Competitive Programming and take part in competitions. Managed an average of 20 people per meeting. As group, we participated in 5 Programming Competitions

list: CONSOLE - Security and training for software supply chain / SME needs
ISSG Hacking Group - training, CTFs, and challenge development

volunteer_experience_full.title: Executive Chair
volunteer_experience_full.org: IEEE Student Branch University of Patras
bullets: Managed 25 volunteers within a 100+ member branch
Organized events, external collaborations and community initiatives

As the Chair of the student branch, I am in charge of organizing and overseeing the executive committee(5 positions) , representing the branch in public events ,managing external affairs and keeping track of the overall progress of the organization .

The Computer Society Chapter also falls under my direct supervision, which consists of 4 scientific groups ( Programming, Competitive Programming, Cyber security, Quantum Computing ). I am in charge of the 4 coordinators, one for each SG, keeping close communication with them, assisting and advising them.

volunteer_experience_full.title: Cybersecurity Study Group Coordinator
bullets: Led sessions for 15 students on vulnerability discovery and exploitation
Prepared hands-on labs and guided practical cybersecurity training

As a coordinator I am in charge of preparing for the meeting, finding useful material and challenges , presenting them , helping the group progress and assign homework to further the group's progress.

In the scientific group we are learning about cybersecurity,identifying vulnerabilities and exploiting them, learning tools and techniques through cybersecurity challenges, such as ctf, tryhackme, hackthebox .

As a coordinator I am in charge of preparing for the meeting, finding useful material and challenges , presenting them , helping the group progress and assign homework to further the group's progress. In the scientific group we are learning about cybersecurity,identifying vulnerabilities and exploiting them, learning tools and techniques through cybersecurity challenges, such as ctf, tryhackme, hackthebox .
